div1.entryFree:
Grossness, 1) bulkiness, bulky appearance: perspicuous even as substance, whose g. little characters sum up, Troil. I, 3, 325.*
2) coarseness, want of refinement and delicacy: drove the g. of the foppery into a received belief, Wiv. V, 5, 131. I will purge thy mortal g. Mids. III, 1, 163. weigh it but with the g. of this age, R3 III, 1, 46.
3) enormity, shocking offence: hiding the g. with fair ornament, Merch. III, 2, 80.
4) stupidity: such impossible passages of g. Tw. III, 2, 77.
